users.tagline: What else?
users.bio: Actor, director, producer. Co-founder of Casamigos tequila. Owns Villa Oleandra on Lake Como. Two-time Sexiest Man Alive. Also won an Oscar somewhere in there.

users.tagline: Build it right or don't build it
users.bio: Actor, producer, architecture enthusiast. Founded Make It Right Foundation to build homes in New Orleans after Katrina. Restores motorcycles. Collects mid-century furniture.

users.tagline: Nothing is impossible, the word itself says I'm possible
users.bio: Actress, humanitarian, fashion icon. UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador. Breakfast at Tiffany's, Roman Holiday. Spoke six languages fluently. Grew tulips in Switzerland.

users.tagline: I coulda been a contender
users.bio: The greatest actor of the 20th century. Method acting pioneer. Bought an island in French Polynesia. Refused the Oscar for The Godfather. Sent Sacheen Littlefeather instead.

users.tagline: Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee
users.bio: Three-time World Heavyweight Champion. Conscientious objector. Lit the Olympic flame in Atlanta 1996. Born Cassius Marcellus Clay Jr. Changed the world inside and outside the ring.

users.tagline: The beautiful game
users.bio: Pele. Three World Cups. 1,283 career goals. The only player to win three FIFA World Cup titles. Started barefoot on the streets of Tres Coracoes. Made football poetry.

users.tagline: A champion is defined not by their wins but by how they recover when they fall
users.bio: 23 Grand Slam singles titles. Grew up in Compton. Father coached her and Venus on public courts. Changed tennis, fashion, and motherhood all at once.

users.tagline: I can accept failure, I can't accept not trying
users.bio: Six NBA championships. Five MVPs. Cut from his high school team. Became the greatest basketball player ever. Air Jordan shoes changed the sneaker industry forever.

users.tagline: Imagination is more important than knowledge
users.bio: E=mc2. Fled Nazi Germany. Patent clerk who rewrote physics over lunch breaks. Nobel Prize 1921. Played violin badly but with feeling. Hair never cooperated.

users.tagline: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood
users.bio: First woman to win a Nobel Prize. Then won a second one. Discovered radium and polonium. Literally glowed in the dark from radiation exposure. Her notebooks are still radioactive.

users.tagline: Look up at the stars and not down at your feet
users.bio: A Brief History of Time sold 25 million copies. Diagnosed with ALS at 21, told he had 2 years. Lived to 76. Proved black holes radiate. Spoke through a computer but said more than most.

users.tagline: It always seems impossible until it's done
users.bio: 27 years in prison. Became president. Forgave his jailers. United a nation that was trying to tear itself apart. The most patient revolutionary who ever lived.

users.tagline: Be the change you wish to see in the world
users.bio: Led India to independence without firing a shot. Salt March. Non-violence as a weapon. Spun his own cloth. Proved that the meek can inherit the earth if they're stubborn enough.

users.tagline: I have a dream
users.bio: I Have a Dream. Letter from Birmingham Jail. Nobel Peace Prize at 35. Assassinated at 39. Changed America's conscience. The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice.

users.tagline: The most effective way to do it is to do it
users.bio: First woman to fly solo across the Atlantic. Set speed and altitude records. Disappeared over the Pacific in 1937. Never found. Still flying somewhere.

users.tagline: I did not tell half of what I saw
users.bio: Traveled the Silk Road to China at age 17. Served Kublai Khan for 17 years. Wrote Il Milione from a Genoese prison. The original digital nomad, minus the digital.

users.tagline: The sea is dangerous but it is not the end
users.bio: First circumnavigation of the Earth. Killed in the Philippines but his crew finished the job. Proved the world was round the hard way. 3 years, 18 surviving crew out of 270.

users.tagline: I will not be triumphed over
users.bio: Last pharaoh of Egypt. Spoke nine languages. Commanded a navy. Seduced two Roman rulers. Died on her own terms. Not actually Egyptian -- she was Macedonian Greek.

users.tagline: Be water, my friend
users.bio: Martial artist, philosopher, filmmaker. Created Jeet Kune Do. Enter the Dragon. Died at 32 but lived more in those years than most do in 80. Water doesn't fight, it flows.

users.tagline: I paint my own reality
users.bio: 143 paintings, 55 self-portraits. Survived polio, a bus accident, 30+ surgeries. Married Diego Rivera twice. Turned suffering into art. The original unfiltered selfie queen.

users.tagline: What I cannot create, I do not understand
users.bio: Nobel Prize physics. Cracked safes at Los Alamos for fun. Played bongo drums in a strip club. Drew physics diagrams on napkins. The most entertaining genius who ever lived.

users.tagline: Science and everyday life cannot and should not be separated
users.bio: Took the X-ray photograph that revealed DNA's double helix. Watson and Crick used her data without credit. Died at 37 from ovarian cancer. The most robbed scientist in history.

users.tagline: Stay hungry, stay foolish
users.bio: Started Apple in a garage. Got fired from his own company. Came back and built the iPod, iPhone, iPad. One more thing. Think different. Wore the same outfit every day.

users.tagline: Never trust a computer you can't throw out a window
users.bio: Built the Apple I and Apple II by hand. Jobs sold them. The real engineer behind Apple. Still teaches kids to code. Plays Segway polo. The most lovable nerd alive.

users.tagline: Carry out a random act of kindness with no expectation of reward
users.bio: Princess of Wales. Walked through active minefields in Angola. Shook hands with AIDS patients when nobody would. The queen of hearts. Died in Paris, 1997.

users.display_name: Galileo di Vincenzo Bonaiuti de Galilei
users.tagline: And yet it moves
users.bio: Father of observational astronomy. Dropped balls off the Leaning Tower of Pisa. Saw Jupiter's moons through a telescope he built himself. The Church said stop. He said eppur si muove.

users.tagline: Fashion fades, only style remains the same
users.bio: Coco Chanel. Freed women from corsets. Created the little black dress, Chanel No. 5, the tweed suit. Grew up in an orphanage. Built an empire from nothing. Elegance is refusal.

users.tagline: It is not the strongest that survives but the most adaptable
users.bio: On the Origin of Species. Sailed on HMS Beagle for 5 years. Studied finches in the Galapagos. Sat on his theory for 20 years because he knew it would cause hell. It did.

users.tagline: I freed a thousand slaves. I could have freed a thousand more if only they knew they were slaves
users.bio: Escaped slavery, went back 13 times, rescued 70 people. Served as a Union spy in the Civil War. Carried a revolver. Never lost a passenger. The original conductor.

users.tagline: Write drunk, edit sober
users.bio: The Old Man and the Sea. For Whom the Bell Tolls. Nobel Prize 1954. War correspondent, bullfight aficionado, deep sea fisherman. Wrote standing up. Drank sitting down.

users.tagline: That's one small step for man
users.bio: First human to walk on the Moon. July 20, 1969. Eagle Scout, Navy pilot, test pilot, astronaut, professor. After the Moon, he went back to farming in Ohio. Peak humility.

users.tagline: I would like to be remembered as a person who wanted to be free
users.bio: Refused to give up her seat on a Montgomery bus, December 1, 1955. Sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott. Quiet courage. One woman, one seat, one revolution.

users.tagline: In the future everyone will be world-famous for fifteen minutes
users.bio: Andy Warhol. Campbell's Soup Cans. Marilyn Monroe silkscreens. The Factory. Interview magazine. Shot by Valerie Solanas, survived. Turned commerce into art and art into commerce.

users.tagline: One child, one teacher, one pen, and one book can change the world
users.bio: Shot by the Taliban at 15 for wanting an education. Survived. Became the youngest Nobel Peace Prize laureate at 17. Founded the Malala Fund. Still going to school.

users.tagline: Every tile tells a story of a thousand years
users.bio: Fourth-generation zellige tile maker from the Tunis medina. Hand-cuts every piece. Teaches traditional Islamic geometric patterns. One of the last masters.

users.tagline: The blade remembers every hammer strike
users.bio: Japanese swordsmith, 5th generation. Forges traditional katanas using tamahagane steel. One of 300 licensed swordsmiths in Japan. Each blade takes 3 months.

users.tagline: Cloth is history you can wear
users.bio: Master Kente weaver from the Ashanti region. Uses traditional Ashanti loom. Each pattern has a name and meaning. Taught by his grandfather at age 8.

users.tagline: Nature already has the answer, we just forgot the question
users.bio: Ethnobotanist specializing in medicinal plants of the Andes. Catalogued 200+ species used by indigenous healers. Runs community herb gardens in Cusco.

users.tagline: Listen to the bees, they know more than we do
users.bio: Third-generation beekeeper from the Altai Mountains. Manages 200 hives using traditional methods. Her bees have never been treated with chemicals. Makes mead on the side.

users.tagline: Wood sings when you listen
users.bio: Oud maker in Doha. Hand-carves each instrument from walnut and spruce. Studied under masters in Damascus and Cairo. His ouds are played by musicians across the Gulf.

users.tagline: Paper holds memory better than stone
users.bio: Traditional washi paper maker in Echizen. Uses 1500-year-old techniques with kozo bark. Her paper is used for calligraphy, restoration of ancient texts, and art.

users.tagline: Do what you gotta do so you can do what you wanna do
users.bio: Two Oscars. Training Day. Malcolm X. Glory. Fences. The greatest actor of his generation. Fordham graduate. Son of a Pentecostal minister. Never phones it in.

users.tagline: The great gift of human beings is that we have the power of empathy
users.bio: Meryl Streep. 21 Oscar nominations, 3 wins. Can do any accent. Sophie's Choice. The Devil Wears Prada. The Iron Lady. Vassar, Yale Drama. The greatest actress who ever lived.

users.tagline: The ball is my best friend
users.bio: 8 Ballon d'Or. World Cup 2022. Left Rosario at 13 for Barcelona. Growth hormone deficiency. 5'7" and still the best who ever played. Makes the impossible look routine.

users.tagline: I'm not the next Usain Bolt or Michael Phelps. I'm the first Simone Biles
users.bio: Most decorated gymnast in history. 37 Olympic and World Championship medals. Four skills named after her. Walked away from the 2021 Olympics to protect her mental health. Came back and won more.

users.tagline: I don't think limits
users.bio: Fastest human ever. 9.58 seconds in the 100m. 8 Olympic golds. Celebrated before the finish line and still won. From Trelawny Parish, Jamaica. Retired undefeated.

users.tagline: What you do makes a difference, and you have to decide what kind of difference you want to make
users.bio: 60+ years studying chimpanzees in Tanzania. No degree when she started. Proved chimps use tools. UN Messenger of Peace. 90 years old and still fighting for the planet.

users.tagline: The sea, once it casts its spell, holds one in its net of wonder forever
users.bio: Co-invented the Aqua-Lung. Explored every ocean on the Calypso. Made 120 documentaries. Showed the world what lived beneath the waves. The original ocean influencer.

users.tagline: I won't be a rock star, I will be a legend
users.bio: Freddie Mercury. Bohemian Rhapsody. We Will Rock You. Born in Zanzibar, grew up in India, conquered the world from London. Four-octave voice. Died of AIDS, 1991. Is this the real life?

users.tagline: The Analytical Engine weaves algebraic patterns just as the Jacquard loom weaves flowers and leaves
users.bio: Wrote the first computer program in 1843, 100 years before computers existed. Daughter of Lord Byron. Saw that Babbage's engine could do more than math. The first programmer was a woman.
